ch3 EXPAND: kept high-value Fed-conflict spine, Mitchell character core (incl. signature quotes), and the Feb 16 climax; dropped lower-scoring building/society/biography detail to land near 1900w.
ch18 EXPAND: kept arrival, debts, signature diary quotes, Baruch's fortune story, capitalism observation and foreshadow; dropped travelogue and minor scene detail.

ch20 CONTRACT: kept the dramatic peak beats (dawn crowd, the bell, panic, Steel collapse, bankers' pool, Whitney's Steel bid, record close, Hoover line); trimmed the Cockburn lunch digression, Livermore, the astrologer, Durant/Berengaria and procedural scene-setting.
ch24 EXPAND: kept Mellon liquidationism, Hearst/Hoover statements, Glass-vs-Mitchell climax and the Durant coda; dropped procedural meeting logistics and secondary letters.

ch27 CONTRACT to ~330: kept only the core Depression-framing (the relentless unraveling, the credit collapse, 'the Great Depression had begun', the dramatic transition); dropped Hoover-market, Keynes, Smoot-Hawley and gold-standard policy detail.

ch28 EXPAND: kept the Lamont birthday frame, the private deflation verdict, 'slower than death', the changing Street, and the Sherrod/Scapegoats explosion; dropped the Josephson/Tarbell/Time press-history detour.
ch29 EXPAND to ~290: kept the exposure of Raskob's anti-Hoover smear, the 'all Raskob's fault' editorial, his on-air rebuttal and the blame-shift; dropped radio logistics and policy proposals.

ch37 CONTRACT to ~640: kept the Hoover/FDR confrontation core (tea ambush, 'No, no, no!', emergency-powers request, 'Like hell I will!', 'close them, of course', the inaugural indictment); dropped Lamont letters, call logs and the bank-reopening procedural tail.
